name: São Jacinto and Estarreja — circular route from Aveiro
name_original: São Jacinto e Estarreja — percurso circular a partir de Aveiro
region_intro: Road cycling around Esgueira is characterized by its notably flat topography, making it suitable for a wide range of cyclists. The region is situated near the Ria de Aveiro lagoon, offering routes that traverse its banks and diverse ecosystems. Landscapes include salt marshes, pine forests, and traditional farming fields. This area provides extensive cycling routes with minimal elevation changes. Best road cycling routes around Esgueira The most popular road cycling route is Ria de Aveiro Great Route Loop, a 27.3 miles (44.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 51 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the unique lagoon landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Aveiro and Praia de Mira via EuroVelo 1, a moderate 44.8 miles (72.2 km) path. This route connects to the EuroVelo 1 network and features coastal scenery. Local road cyclists also love the From Aveiro to Praia da Vagueira — route in Ria de Aveiro, a 27.5 miles (44.2 km) trail leading through the Ria de Aveiro, often completed in about 1 hour 57 minutes. Road cycling around Esgueira is defined by the Ria de Aveiro lagoon, coastal areas, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels. The routes in Esgueira are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 2000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Esgueira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Esgueira?
A: There are over 100 road cycling routes available around Esgueira, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a diverse selection, from easy rides to more challenging excursions.

Q: What is the typical terrain and elevation like for road cycling in Esgueira?
A: The region around Esgueira is notably flat, making it ideal for road cycling without significant elevation changes. Routes often traverse the banks of the Ria de Aveiro lagoon, offering smooth rides through picturesque landscapes.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes in Esgueira?
A: Esgueira offers a range of difficulty levels. You'll find approximately 19 easy routes, 73 moderate routes, and 12 more difficult options, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I expect to see while cycling around Esgueira?
A: While cycling, you can enjoy the unique lagoon landscapes of the Ria de Aveiro, including salt marshes and traditional 'moliceiro' boats. Routes also pass through pine forests and farming fields. Notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3466981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barra Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; and the charming striped houses of Praia da Costa Nova.

Q: Are there any unique cultural sights along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, beyond natural beauty, you can encounter cultural elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3893934" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moliceiro Boats on the Canals of Aveiro&lt;/a&gt; are a distinctive sight, and the traditional striped houses of Costa Nova add a unique architectural charm to the coastal areas.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the Esgueira area?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383489752/ria-de-aveiro-great-route-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ria de Aveiro Great Route Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers extensive views of the lagoon. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383559697/sao-jacinto-and-estarreja-circular-route-from-aveiro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;São Jacinto and Estarreja — circular route from Aveiro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options around Esgueira?
A: Absolutely. The flat topography of the Esgueira region makes it very suitable for family-friendly cycling. Many of the 19 easy routes are perfect for families looking for a relaxed ride without challenging climbs.

Q: What are the options for parking and public transport when planning a road cycling trip to Esgueira?
A: Esgueira and the nearby city of Aveiro are well-connected. While specific parking details for each route vary, Aveiro offers various parking facilities. Public transport, such as trains and buses, can also be used to reach the area, often with options for transporting bicycles, though it's advisable to check specific operator policies.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Esgueira?
A: The region is generally pleasant for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer mild temperatures and fewer crowds, making them ideal. Summer can be warm but is still popular, especially along the coastal routes where sea breezes provide relief.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Esgueira?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the flat, accessible terrain, the stunning views of the Ria de Aveiro, and the well-maintained routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383495080/aveiro-and-praia-de-mira-via-eurovelo-1" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aveiro and Praia de Mira via EuroVelo 1&lt;/a&gt;, which connects to the EuroVelo 1 network.

Q: Can I find cafes or accommodation along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, especially in and around Aveiro and along popular coastal stretches like Praia da Costa Nova, you'll find various cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. Many routes pass through or near towns where you can refuel and rest.

Q: Are there routes that connect Esgueira to other towns or coastal areas?
A: Definitely. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383476232/from-aveiro-to-praia-da-vagueira-route-in-ria-de-aveiro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Aveiro to Praia da Vagueira&lt;/a&gt; offer connections to other coastal towns. The region's network, including parts of the European Coastal Path (GR11-E9), allows for longer journeys connecting various points of interest.
tips: Former Jerónimo Pereira Campos Ceramics Factory, built between 1915 and 1917. The red brick facade and its central chimney are postcards of Aveiro, which highlight the beauty of the canal. It was deactivated in the 1960s and renovated in 1995, becoming the Aveiro Congress Center. Today you can find the IEFP facilities here and some old parts from the factory on display.

Ferry terminal operated by Aveiro Bus to São Jacinto. Only the cyclist pays, not the bike. Rates and times below:
https://www.aveirobus.pt/transporte-fluvial
Cute fishing community on the extreme south end of the narrow sandy peninsula with great bike trails along the waterfront esplanade, dotted with small cafes and restaurants.
Alternative road parallel to EN 109 that offers a few kilometers quieter. Furthermore, it runs through corn fields, making the landscape even more picturesque.

Estarreja is known for the Ria de Aveiro, the annual Carnival and Foral do Antuã of D. Manuel I and for its unique urban art.

At the ESTAU Festival, started by the artist Bordallo II, artists from all over the world transform the city with murals and installations inspired by local culture. In the link below you can find all the murals in Estarreja:
https://www.cm-estarreja.pt/estau
